recentJobs.title: Product architecture note, PRD, and presentation

suggestedQueries: Across the corpus, what are the strongest recurring mechanisms for durable knowledge accumulation?
What should be primary, secondary, and supporting in a production LLM Wiki architecture?
Which parts of the corpus are most relevant to building a graph-assisted but wiki-first system?
What are the biggest integrity risks if the wiki becomes heavily automated?

reviewQueue.summary: New architecture note translating product thesis into modules, state layout, and runtime design.
Catalog entry added so the new architecture note becomes part of the durable wiki navigation layer.
Chronological record of the architecture-writing query and the files it touched.

wiki/outputs/2026-04-07-llm-wiki-repo-an.text: + # 2026-04-07 LLM Wiki Repo and App Architecture
+ The cleanest implementation is a repo-backed workspace plus a thin local control app.
+ The compiler should be incremental, not full rebuild by default.

index.md.text: - [2026-04-07 LLM Wiki Presentation](wiki/outputs/2026-04-07-llm-wiki-presentation.md): A Marp slide deck presenting the LLM Wiki pattern, architecture, and MVP.

+ - [2026-04-07 LLM Wiki Repo and App Architecture](wiki/outputs/2026-04-07-llm-wiki-repo-and-app-architecture.md): A concrete implementation architecture describing the workspace layout, runtime modules, job model, and app shape for building an actual LLM Wiki tool.

log.md.text: ## [2026-04-07] query | Turn the architecture synthesis into product artifacts
+ ## [2026-04-07] query | Turn the product architecture note into an actual repo and app architecture
+ Summary: Created a concrete implementation architecture for building LLM Wiki as a repo-backed workspace plus local control app.
